commit.message:
feat: add search_concepts and search_patterns MCP tools (Priority 3)

Adds two new tools to the MCP server: search_concepts (Fuse.js search over concept titles/descriptions) and search_patterns (Fuse.js search over pattern titles/descriptions/difficulty). Previously, LLMs had to know the exact slug to retrieve concept or pattern content. Now they can discover relevant guides by keyword. Updates the /mcp docs page to list all 10 tools.

Migrate editor from TipTap to BlockNote

Replace TipTap HTML editor with BlockNote's JSON block-based editor
for a richer editing experience (slash menu, drag handles, formatting
toolbar) and an AI-friendly structured storage format.

Frontend:
- Swap 13 TipTap packages for BlockNote + Mantine
- Rewrite NoteEditor with useCreateBlockNote + BlockNoteView
- Delete EditorToolbar (BlockNote has built-in UI)
- Update App.tsx data flow for JSON content format
- Add JSON text utilities (extractPlainText, isBlockNoteEmpty)
- Remove DOMPurify dependency from ChatMessage

Backend:
- Add NoteContentHelper to transparently extract plain text from
  both BlockNote JSON and legacy HTML formats
- Inject helper into FactExtractionService, PageService,
  NotebookQueryService, DeterministicRoutingService, NoteService
- Increase rawText column limits for JSON storage (V2 migration)
- Update test constructors for new service signatures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🔒 feat: input replay anti-cheat for browser leaderboard

Replace client-authoritative HMAC-based anti-cheat with server-side
wave replay. The client records tower actions (place/sell/move) with
frame numbers during each wave; the server replays them through
simulateWaveWithActions() to compute the authoritative score. The
client never sends a score — the server owns it.

- Add WaveAction types, BrowserSessionState, and ActionRecorder
- Add simulateWaveWithActions() alongside existing simulateWave()
- New API endpoints: place-tower, sell-tower, move-tower, start-wave
- Rewrite wave-complete to replay action logs server-side
- Rewrite submit-score to read score from server session (no HMAC)
- GameScene: fixed-timestep accumulator (1/60s), action recording,
  server state sync on wave complete, death wave reporting
- GameOverScene: simplified submission (sessionId + initials only)
- Agent API (api/game/*) completely untouched

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: rename campaigns table to meta_campaigns and wire up creative library picker

Bug 1: The original migration used CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS campaigns, silently
colliding with the existing content-planner campaigns table. This migration creates
public.meta_campaigns with RLS, and updates all .from('campaigns') references in
campaign actions and the cron sync route to .from('meta_campaigns').

Bug 2: The 'Pick creative from library' button had no onClick handler and the
component had no access to library data. This wires up:
- AiCampaignPayload.ad_sets[].ads[] gains image_url and media_asset_id fields
- NewCampaignPage fetches mediaLibrary via listMediaAssets() and passes it down
- CampaignBriefForm accepts and forwards mediaLibrary to CampaignTree
- CampaignTree renders an inline image grid picker that updates the ad payload
- AdPreview already accepted imageUrl — now passed from the selected ad state
- saveCampaignDraft persists media_asset_id when saving the draft

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Opaque types: export type without exposing constructors

`export opaque type Email = Email String` exports the type name for use
in annotations but hides constructors from importers, preventing direct
construction, pattern matching, and field access. Consumers must use
exported smart constructors and accessors instead.

Works with both ADTs and records. Completes all planned language goals.

commit.message:
Disable libssh2 in curl build — not needed for HTTP-only extension

curl's CMake defaults CURL_USE_LIBSSH2 to ON, which creates a
runtime dependency on libssh2 when it's found on the build host.
This extension only does HTTP/HTTPS, so disable SCP/SFTP support
to keep the built artifact self-contained.

emulator namespaces. Sign in as each user before writing their sessions
to satisfy _flushedBy rule. Seed now creates systems/{system} with
offices, rotations, and role maps. CSP connect-src adds *.firebaseapp.com.

feat(migrations): add codigo_ean column to cadastro_palletizacao

- Introduced a new migration script to add the codigo_ean column to the cadastro_palletizacao table, enabling the association of EAN/GTIN products from the Atacadao portal.
- Created an accompanying SQL file for idempotent execution of the migration.
- Included checks before and after the migration to ensure the column and index are correctly created.

fix(ios): two-phase startup to prevent infinite loading on first launch

Split restoreSession() into fast auth check (Phase 1) + background data
loading (Phase 2). Add 15s request timeout to URLSession. App now shows
MainTabView within 1-2s instead of hanging indefinitely when network is slow.

commit.message:
feat: add LICENSE, SECURITY.md, Mermaid diagrams; fix shell injection

LICENSE
- MIT license under Shyam Jayachandran (2026)

SECURITY.md
- Responsible disclosure policy
- Scope, known limitations, out-of-scope items

README.md
- Replace ASCII flow with three Mermaid diagrams:
  loop flowchart, agent architecture graph, runtime wiring
- Fix stale agentStop references → sessionEnd throughout

Security fixes (scripts/)
- Pass SESSION_ID, AGENT, TS, COMMENT, USER_FEEDBACK to Python
  via os.environ instead of string interpolation — eliminates
  shell/Python injection via untrusted input
- Add AGENT allowlist validation ([a-z-]+ only) in both
  run-critique.sh and submit-feedback.sh to prevent path traversal
- Extract CLUSTER_JSON and EVIDENCE via env var to avoid
  single-quote injection in Python one-liners

All tests pass.

GitHub repos, replacing fake hashes with real ones in the timeline.

Strip all Lovable references from README, index.html, and vite config.

Fix FEATURE SEQUENCE step numbering; add outer loop, DevOps design check to session-insession.md

- Step 0 (outer loop / lean check) added before step 1
- Step 2 (DevOps design check) inserted between scope confirm and DDD RED
- Steps renumbered: DDD RED=4, PRE-IMPL=5, Gherkin=6, Approval=7, TDD=8
- THREE LOOPS section rewritten: product feedback loop outermost, lean/systems thinking frame
- Outer loop principles summary: Ries, Adzic, AARRR, CD3, Gilb/Hubbard, DORA
- DevOps design check sequence added: single responsibility, bounded interface, detectable failure
- Added missing sources: Beck, Poppendieck, Meadows/Senge, Norman/Krug/Christensen
- Waste log trigger: any new persistent artefact → DevOps design check

red dots on icons when collapsed.

Restore player session on page refresh — skip landing page

When a player refreshes the browser mid-game, their session is now
automatically restored from localStorage (teamtrack_last_session).
The app verifies the team still exists and the device is a member,
then reconnects teamSync and jumps straight back into the game.

- Read teamtrack_last_session on init (24h expiry, team verification)
- Restore activeGameId, mode=PLAY, teamSync, currentTeam, soloMode
- Auto-open playground for playzone/aroundtheworld games
- Clear session on explicit Leave Game
- Players no longer see the landing page on refresh

commit.message:
Add Platform Admin dashboard with feature toggles, pricing, health monitoring

- Backend: platform_admin module (models, schemas, service, router)
- FeatureFlag, PlatformSetting, ErrorLog database tables
- Super admin access via SUPER_ADMIN_EMAILS env var or ADMIN role
- Dashboard: system metrics, users by role, activity chart, recent activity
- Users: search, filter, detail panel, impersonation, session revocation
- Feature toggles: 18 default flags organized by category with toggle UI
- Pricing & limits: editable plan prices, O-Brain tiers, add-ons, usage limits
- API keys: masked key display with per-field configuration status
- Health: system status, uptime, integration health, error/warning counts
- Security: active sessions management, paginated audit log
- Errors: error log with resolve functionality
- Seed defaults on startup for feature flags and pricing settings
- Frontend: PlatformAdminPage with 8-tab sidebar navigation

feat: Switch default orchestrator LLM to Haiku (#47)

Default backend changes from local llama-server to Anthropic API with
claude-haiku-4-5-latest. Uses undated alias so new versions are picked
up automatically. Model is configurable via LIFEOS_ANTHROPIC_MODEL.

Specialist calls (web_search, person_facts, etc.) remain pinned to
Sonnet via get_anthropic_llm().

Closes #46

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add exhibit state and nested state region support to simulator

The state parser now extracts state machines from three sources:
- state def X { ... } (existing)
- exhibit state X { ... } inside part def bodies (new)
- Nested state regions like state operatingStates { ... } (new)

Also handles initial pseudo-transitions (transition X then Y; with
no first keyword) as entry-state-setting transitions.

Adds 3 new tests covering exhibit states, parallel regions, and
the annex-a operatingStates pattern.

`--yes`/`--no-verify` under "Automation" in `wt switch`, `wt remove`,
and `wt merge` help output, using clap's `help_heading`.

feat(mcp): add missing tools for full API parity

Add 6 new MCP tools:
- ant_get_session — fetch single session by ID
- ant_update_session — rename a session
- ant_delete_session — delete session and its PTY
- ant_delete_message — delete a conversation message
- ant_kill_all_terminals — nuclear kill of all PTY/tmux sessions
- ant_list_resume_commands — list captured CLI resume commands
- ant_delete_resume_command — remove a captured resume command

Total MCP tools: 9 → 16, now covering all REST API endpoints.

commit.message:
fix: prevent stack overflow on large repos and add GitHub auth

Replace Math.min/max spread with loop-based helpers to avoid call stack
overflow on repos with many commits. Dynamically scale camera far plane,
grid fade distance, and orbit max distance for large graphs. Add
GITHUB_TOKEN env var support to increase API rate limit from 60 to 5000/hr.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace MongoDB with SQLite (better-sqlite3 + drizzle-orm)

Eliminates the Docker/MongoDB dependency in favor of a local SQLite file.
Uses better-sqlite3 for the driver and drizzle-orm for type-safe queries,
following the patterns from reputo-org/reputo.
